name: Public Information (PI)
purpose: Reaches the general public with accurate information about A.A. — through media outreach, public events, and the area's response to inquiries.

name: Cooperation with the Professional Community (CPC)
purpose: Provides information about A.A. to professionals who work with alcoholics — doctors, clergy, lawyers, social workers, and others.

name: Treatment
purpose: Coordinates A.A. members carrying the message into hospitals, recovery homes, and treatment facilities — and Bridging the Gap to help newcomers transition into A.A.

name: Corrections
purpose: Supports A.A. service work inside correctional facilities and the Corrections Correspondence Service.

name: Accessibilities
purpose: Helps the Area carry the message to A.A.s with disabilities, the elderly, and the homebound — and ensures area events are accessible.

name: Archives
purpose: Collects and preserves the history of A.A. in Area 09, including minutes, photographs, audio recordings, and member oral histories.

name: Grapevine & La Viña
purpose: Promotes A.A.'s international journals — Grapevine (English) and La Viña (Spanish) — as Conference-approved tools for carrying the message.
meeting_schedule: Last Saturday of the month — 11:00 AM

name: Literature
purpose: Coordinates the distribution of A.A. Conference-approved literature and supports groups in carrying the message through books and pamphlets.

name: Website & Technology
purpose: Maintains the area website, mailing lists, and supporting technology — applying A.A. principles to our online presence.
